description: Agent 5 session, vote, tournament, map selector, and match stats route/status metadata for RmlUi ownership and starter progression tracking.

status_values.starter: Starter RML document exists in Agent 5-owned assets and preserves representative session command/cvar hooks.
status_values.starter_round2: Second-round starter RML document exists and preserves utility/session route hooks from the legacy menu surface.
status_values.starter_round3: Third-round starter RML document exists and preserves session, vote, tournament, map selector, or match stats hooks from the legacy menu surface.
status_values.live_provider: The route consumes live sgame-published state through the native RmlUi cvar/condition/command bridge and has installed OpenGL evidence; broader parity gates remain open.

migration_phase_values.starter: Source RML document and static route metadata exist; runtime, controller, and parity evidence are pending.
migration_phase_values.controller_stub: Controller/data-model stub metadata exists, but live runtime validation is pending.
migration_phase_values.runtime_stub: Runtime smoke/open path exists for this route, but parity evidence is pending.
migration_phase_values.parity_pending: Route opens through RmlUi with live bridge coverage; parity evidence is still incomplete.
migration_phase_values.parity_ready: Route has accepted parity evidence and is ready for cutover consideration.

routes.legacy_surface: src/game/cgame/ui/worr.json menu dm_welcome plus sgame-published welcome cvars

routes.legacy_surface: src/game/cgame/ui/worr.json menu tourney_mapchoices plus tournament map-choice publisher
routes.notes: Live native sgame-published ten-line map-order report with deterministic empty-state copy, conditional rows, and one standardized return affordance. Seeded 960x720 OpenGL evidence was accepted on 2026-07-13; the central migration phase remains controller_stub until localization, navigation, and native renderer parity gates are accepted.

controller_contracts.notes: The registered sgame route command opens the report, and the standardized backplate is its single return affordance.
All ten map-choice rows consume the ui_tourney_mapchoice_line_* cvars published immediately before the route opens.
Native cvar conditions suppress unused rows while preserving a direct-route empty-state fallback.
The fixed ten-row provider renders the complete bounded server-published map order without transferring tournament ownership into cgame.

routes.legacy_surface: src/game/cgame/ui/worr.json menu tourney_veto plus tournament veto publisher
routes.notes: Live native tournament-veto state route for inactive, waiting, and actor-turn branches. Pick and Ban open the shared live ui_list child provider, whose server-owned entries dispatch the validated veto commands. Seeded 960x720 OpenGL evidence was accepted on 2026-07-13; the central migration phase remains controller_stub until destructive action automation and native renderer parity gates are accepted.

controller_contracts.notes: Pick and Ban dispatch registered sgame commands into the live ui_list child route; the standardized backplate is the single return affordance.
The route consumes the complete ui_tourney_veto_* state published by sgame for the current client and veto turn.
Native cvar conditions select exactly the inactive, waiting, or actionable turn state and independently gate Ban when further bans would invalidate the series.

routes.legacy_surface: src/game/cgame/ui/worr.json menu tourney_replay_confirm plus replay command-cvar publisher
routes.notes: Live admin-only destructive replay confirmation reached through the shared live ui_list provider. Sgame publishes the selected game prompt and exact replay command, the warning explains result truncation, No receives safe default focus, and Yes resets the series through the validated native command. Seeded 960x720 OpenGL evidence was accepted on 2026-07-13; the central migration phase remains controller_stub until destructive action automation and native renderer parity gates are accepted.

controller_contracts.notes: No closes the popup safely; Yes dispatches the sgame-published admin replay command and the native handler closes the active menu after applying or reporting the result.
The prompt and exact worr_tourney_replay game-number command are published by sgame immediately before the popup opens.
The native cvar condition exposes the selected-game warning only when sgame has supplied a valid replay prompt.

routes.legacy_surface: src/game/cgame/ui/worr.json menu vote_menu plus session vote publisher

controller_contracts.notes: Yes and No dispatch through the native command listener, while close/back preserves the live sgame vote-menu cleanup command.

routes.notes: Live native sgame-published vote-state and command provider with installed seeded 960x720 OpenGL evidence accepted on 2026-07-13. The central migration phase remains controller_stub until destructive action automation and native renderer parity gates are accepted.

routes.legacy_surface: src/game/cgame/ui/worr.json menu map_selector plus sgame map-selector publisher
routes.notes: Live native server-driven end-of-match map vote with three sgame-published candidates, per-client acknowledgement, a shrinking bar plus numeric seconds remaining, and persistent per-vote dismissal. Seeded 960x720 OpenGL evidence was accepted on 2026-07-13; the central migration phase remains controller_stub until connected vote/finalization automation and native renderer parity gates are accepted.

controller_contracts.notes: Each candidate dispatches its validated zero-based sgame vote command; Back/Escape/Mouse2 run the close-cleanup command and suppress automatic reopen for the current vote.
The native bridge consumes the title prompt, three candidate labels, acknowledgement lines, numeric time remaining, and countdown bar republished by sgame every 200 ms.
Native conditions switch between candidate and acknowledgement states, suppress the empty-state after voting, and keep the countdown visible through zero seconds.
The bounded three-option provider consumes the exact candidate array published by the active server map-selector lifecycle.

routes.legacy_surface: src/game/cgame/ui/worr.json menu match_stats plus match stats publisher
routes.notes: Live sgame provider publishes the player plus nine semantic combat, damage, and accuracy values every second while open; the RmlUi route presents the fixed snapshot with a truthful empty state and one connection-aware close path while legacy line cvars remain available for the JSON fallback.

controller_contracts.notes: The registered intermission-safe open/close commands own per-client refresh state, and the single RmlUi backplate preserves connected cleanup without disconnected unknown-command noise.
Ten semantic cvars expose player identity plus all nine live stat values; sixteen compatibility line cvars continue to feed the legacy JSON fallback.
The live snapshot and direct-route unavailable state are mutually exclusive based on the server-published player value.
The bounded fixed-record provider groups the authoritative snapshot into Combat, Damage, and Accuracy cards without client-side recomputation.
